---

Great salespeople know that the most important sale they will make in their life is to themselves. They also know that that sale, their product, their service, their offer themselves, their company, what they have, they know they have to keep making that sale over to themselves. That most important sale over to themselves at least every week and hey maybe every day. For instance, I write my goals down every morning. Why am I doing that? Why am I right? You think I forgot them? I wrote them down in the morning and at night. I wrote them down last night at 9 o'clock, went to sleep, woke up at 5.30. Why am I writing them down again? You think I forgot? No, because I'm trying to resell myself on my purpose, my mission, my duty. Why do I have a weekly meeting where all we go over is the successes of the organization? And regardless of how many people I have in that meeting when I say, tell me about business successes, client successes, why am I asking for this? To stay sold on my product and my offering. I own the company. I know what the product is. I am completely convinced. Why do I have a meeting every Monday and say, hey, tell me, who's doing great with it? Who's killing with us? And no matter how big that staff is, we're all like, I don't know, I don't know. It takes us a little while to finally say, oh, Johnny is, Bob is, this person is, this family did this, this guy in Hungary did this, this guy in China did this, this guy in Russia did this, this guy in Brazil did this, this guy in New York and Tennessee. See, all of a sudden everybody's like, oh my god, we got a great product here. The most important sale that you will ever make to yourself is to yourself. The most important sale you can make in your lifetime is not a thousand units of anything but one unit to yourself about you, your product, your company, and your offering. Stay sold.
